message.content:
Here’s a gentle bedtime story perfect for little Wade:

Wade and the Sleepy Forest Friends

Once upon a time, in a quiet, sun-dappled forest, there lived a tiny bunny named Wade. Wade loved to hop through the soft grass and peek at all the animals he met along the way.

One warm evening, as the golden sun began to sink behind the trees, Wade noticed his friends getting sleepy. First, there was Oliver the owl, blinking slowly in his tree. Then came Millie the mouse, curling up in her cozy little burrow. Even Freddie the fox yawned a big, fluffy yawn.

Wade wanted to make sure everyone felt calm and happy before bedtime. So he softly hopped from friend to friend, whispering, “Good night, sleep tight.”

When Wade reached the pond, he found Lucy the little duck paddling quietly. “It’s time to rest, Lucy,” Wade said, “Tomorrow will be a new day of adventures.” Lucy quacked softly, snuggled into the reeds, and closed her eyes.

Finally, Wade curled up under a tall oak tree. He felt the cool grass beneath him, heard the gentle rustle of leaves, and watched the stars twinkle above. As the forest grew still, Wade yawned a tiny yawn, snuggled his paws, and drifted into dreams filled with hopping, waddling, and playful adventures with all his friends.

And in the forest, under the moon’s soft glow, all the animals slept peacefully… including little Wade.

The End.

If you like, I can also make a shorter, rhythmic version with lots of animal sounds that’s extra fun for a 10-month-old to hear before sleep. It would be perfect for reading aloud with little noises like “hop, hop” and “quack, quack.”

Do you want me to make that version too?
